code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test02-NOV-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, indicating possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. Low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal involvement. Low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect recent changes in glucose control or analytical interference. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying metabolic or renal disorders.
